RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 201-F, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Cathy Jo Loy, a Republican, proudly represents part of Jasper County (District 163) in the Missouri House of Representatives. She was elected to her first two-year term in November 2024.
In addition to her legislative duties, Loy has a diverse background as a graphic designer and display artist. She previously ran a small art and event business and was also a licensed home daycare provider. Appointed by Governor Parson, she served for four years on the Missouri Public Charter School Commission, where she worked to improve educational opportunities across the state.
Loy is actively involved in her community. She and her husband, Clint, are members of Christ the King Church in Joplin. Together, they have raised five children and are now the proud grandparents of ten grandchildren. They also serve as guardians to a special needs adult, which has given Loy firsthand insight into the difficulties faced by families in accessing support services.
Loy has called Missouri home for most of her life. She believes that government has overgrown its intended purpose and is committed to advocating for a more limited and efficient government that truly serves the needs of its people.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes the "Missouri Religious Freedom Protection Act"
Establishes the "Missouri Parental Choice Tax Credit Act" relating to a tax credit for certain educational expenses
Specifies that certain medical titles or specialty designations are reserved for the use of licensed physicians
Modifies provisions relating to minimum prison terms
Approves the statewide mission designation expansion for Missouri Southern State University
Proposes a constitutional amendment changing how judges are selected to certain courts
Proposes a constitutional amendment relating to modification of the Constitution

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to income tax
Establishes the "Safe Place for Newborns Fund" for the installation of newborn safety incubators
Modifies standards for storage and use of gold and silver
Requires instruction on human sexuality and development in schools
Requires the general assembly to approve proposed administrative rules
Prohibits state departments from spending money on diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives
Establishes provisions governing duties and responsibilities of public entities
Provides protections against discrimination and antisemitism in public schools and public postsecondary educational institutions
Modifies provisions relating to the list of diseases required to be reported to the department of health and senior services
Creates provisions relating to staffing requirements for emergency departments in hospitals
Prohibits a political subdivision from adopting policies for property transferred by political subdivisions from being used for lawful education purposes by a charter school
Prohibits a political subdivision from adopting policies for property transferred by political subdivisions from being used for lawful education purposes by a charter school
Prohibits a political subdivision from adopting policies for property transferred by political subdivisions from being used for lawful education purposes by a charter school
Designates the last week in April each year as "Infertility Awareness Week"

Proposes a constitutional amendment to impose an appropriation spending limitation and to establish the "Tax Reform Fund" to be used to fund budgetary shortfalls, subject to an appropriation limitation, implement sales tax changes, and allow for certain taxation changes based on revenue triggers, by general law
Proposes a constitutional amendment to assert the right of parents to participate in and direct the education of their children free from government interference
